sourceQuestion:
Seven machines take 7 minutes to make 7 identical toys. At the same rate, how many minutes would it take for 1 machine to make 7 toys?

A. 8  B. 7  C. 100  D. 700

parsed.stem: Seven machines take 7 minutes to make 7 identical toys. At the same rate, how many minutes would it take for 1 machine to make 7 toys?

sourceQuestion:
A contract is to be completed in 52 days and 125 identical robots were employed, each operational for 7 hours a day. After 39 days, five-sevenths of the work was completed. How many additional robots would be required to complete the work on time, if each robot is now operational for 8 hours a day?

A. 50  B. 89  C. 148  D. 175

parsed.stem: A contract is to be completed in 52 days and 125 identical robots were employed, each operational for 7 hours a day. After 39 days, five-sevenths of the work was completed. How many additional robots would be required to complete the work on time, if each robot is now operational for 8 hours a day?

sourceQuestion:
An oil tank can be filled by pipe X in 5 hours and pipe Y in 4 hours, each pump working on its own. When the oil tank is full and the drainage hole is open, the oil is drained in 20 hours. If initially the tank was empty and someone started the two pipes together but left the drainage hole open, how many hours will it take for the tank to be filled? (Assume that the rate of drainage is independent of the amount of oil in the tank.)

A. 1.50  B. 2.00  C. 2.50  D. 4.00

parsed.stem: An oil tank can be filled by pipe X in 5 hours and pipe Y in 4 hours, each pump working on its own. When the oil tank is full and the drainage hole is open, the oil is drained in 20 hours. If initially the tank was empty and someone started the two pipes together but left the drainage hole open, how many hours will it take for the tank to be filled? (Assume that the rate of drainage is independent of the amount of oil in the tank.)

sourceQuestion:
It would take one machine 4 hours to complete a production order and another machine 2 hours to complete the same order. If both machines work simultaneously at their respective constant rates, the time taken to complete the same order is ________ hours.

A. 2/3
B. 3/4
C. 4/3
D. 7/3

parsed.stem: It would take one machine 4 hours to complete a production order and another machine 2 hours to complete the same order. If both machines work simultaneously at their respective constant rates, the time taken to complete the same order is ________ hours.

sourceQuestion:
It takes two hours for a person X to mow the lawn. Y can mow the same lawn in four hours. How long (in minutes) will it take X and Y, if they work together to mow the lawn?

A. 60
B. 80
C. 90
D. 120

parsed.stem: It takes two hours for a person X to mow the lawn. Y can mow the same lawn in four hours. How long (in minutes) will it take X and Y, if they work together to mow the lawn?

sourceQuestion:
Two pipes P and Q can fill a tank in 9 hours and 6 hours respectively, while a third pipe R can empty the tank in 12 hours. Initially, P and R are open for 6 hours. Then P is closed and Q is opened. After 6 more hours R is closed. The total time taken to fill the tank (in hours) is ____

A. 13.50
B. 14.00
C. 15.50
D. 14.50

parsed.stem: Two pipes P and Q can fill a tank in 9 hours and 6 hours respectively, while a third pipe R can empty the tank in 12 hours. Initially, P and R are open for 6 hours. Then P is closed and Q is opened. After 6 more hours R is closed. The total time taken to fill the tank (in hours) is ____

sourceQuestion: A graph is planar if and only if, A. It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to K_5 and K_{3,3}. B. It does not contain a subgraph isomorphic to K_5 and K_{3,3}. C. It does not contain a subgraph isomorphic to K_5 or K_{3,3}. D. It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to K_5 or K_{3,3}.
sourceAnswer: D. It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to K_5 or K_{3,3}.

parsed.stem: A graph is planar if and only if,

options.text: It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to K_5 and K_{3,3}.
It does not contain a subgraph isomorphic to K_5 and K_{3,3}.
It does not contain a subgraph isomorphic to K_5 or K_{3,3}.
It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to K_5 or K_{3,3}.

parsed.stem: A non-planar graph with minimum number of vertices has

options.text: 9 edges, 6 vertices
6 edges, 4 vertices
10 edges, 5 vertices
9 edges, 5 vertices

sourceQuestion: Let G be a simple connected planar graph with 13 vertices and 19 edges. Then, the number of faces in the planar embedding of the graph is: A. 6 B. 8 C. 9 D. 13

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following sets of pieces can be assembled to form a square with a single round hole near the center? Pieces cannot overlap.

A. [Four irregular interlocking pieces with a circular cutout among them]
B. [Four irregular pieces that do not combine into a complete square]
C. [Four irregular interlocking pieces that fit together to form a perfect square with a round hole at center]
D. [Four pieces with mismatched edges that leave gaps when assembled]
sourceAnswer: C. The set of pieces in option C assembles into a square with a single round hole near the center

parsed.stem: Which one of the following sets of pieces can be assembled to form a square with a single round hole near the center? Pieces cannot overlap.

options.text: [Four irregular interlocking pieces with a circular cutout among them]
[Four irregular pieces that do not combine into a complete square]
[Four irregular interlocking pieces that fit together to form a perfect square with a round hole at center]
[Four pieces with mismatched edges that leave gaps when assembled]

sourceQuestion:
A jigsaw puzzle has 2 pieces. One of the pieces is shown above. Which one of the given options for the missing piece when assembled will form a rectangle? The piece can be moved, rotated or flipped to assemble with the above piece.

A. [A piece with interlocking notches that is the mirror complement of the given piece]
B. [A piece with a different notch pattern that does not complement the given piece]
C. [A piece with extra protrusions that overlap the given piece]
D. [A piece whose straight edge does not match the step-cut profile of the given piece]
sourceAnswer: A. The piece shown in option A is the missing piece that completes the rectangle

parsed.stem: A jigsaw puzzle has 2 pieces. One of the pieces is shown above. Which one of the given options for the missing piece when assembled will form a rectangle? The piece can be moved, rotated or flipped to assemble with the above piece.

options.text: [A piece with interlocking notches that is the mirror complement of the given piece]
[A piece with a different notch pattern that does not complement the given piece]
[A piece with extra protrusions that overlap the given piece]
[A piece whose straight edge does not match the step-cut profile of the given piece]

sourceQuestion:
A jigsaw puzzle has 2 pieces. One of the pieces is shown above (an irregular interlocking shape with notches on one side). Which one of the given options for the missing piece when assembled will form a rectangle? The piece can be moved, rotated or flipped to assemble with the above piece.

A. (piece with two upward bumps and a flat left edge)
B. (piece with bumps and notches mirrored differently)
C. (piece with bumps on the right side)
D. (piece with bumps and a different notch pattern)
sourceAnswer: A. (piece with two upward bumps whose notches exactly complement the given piece to fill a rectangle)

parsed.stem: A jigsaw puzzle has 2 pieces. One of the pieces is shown above (an irregular interlocking shape with notches on one side). Which one of the given options for the missing piece when assembled will form a rectangle? The piece can be moved, rotated or flipped to assemble with the above piece.

options.text: (piece with two upward bumps and a flat left edge)
(piece with bumps and notches mirrored differently)
(piece with bumps on the right side)
(piece with bumps and a different notch pattern)

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the groups given below can be assembled to get the shape that is shown above (a tall arrow/shield silhouette) using each piece only once without overlapping with each other? (rotation and translation operations may be used).

A. (group: 2 large upward triangles, 1 small square, 1 downward triangle)
B. (group: 2 large upward triangles, 1 small square, 2 smaller upward triangles)
C. (group: 1 large upward triangle, 1 small square, 1 large downward-right triangle, 1 small upward triangle)
D. (group: 1 large upward triangle, 1 small square, 1 downward triangle, 2 upward triangles)

(group: 1 large upward triangle, 1 small square, 1 large downward-right triangle, 1 small upward triangle)

sourceQuestion:
The figure shows a grid formed by a collection of unit squares. The unshaded unit square in the grid represents a hole. What is the maximum number of squares without a 'hole in the interior' that can be formed within the 4 x 4 grid using the unit squares as building blocks?

A. 15    B. 20    C. 21    D. 26

parsed.stem: The figure shows a grid formed by a collection of unit squares. The unshaded unit square in the grid represents a hole. What is the maximum number of squares without a 'hole in the interior' that can be formed within the 4 x 4 grid using the unit squares as building blocks?

sourceQuestion:
In the 4 x 4 array shown below, each cell of the first three columns has either a cross (X) or a number, as per the given rule.

Rule: The number in a cell represents the count of crosses around its immediate neighboring cells (left, right, top, bottom, diagonals).

The array (rows top to bottom, columns left to right) is:
Row 1: 1 | 1 | 2 | ?
Row 2: 1 | X | 3 | ?
Row 3: 2 | X | 4 | ?
Row 4: X | 1 | X | ?

As per this rule, the maximum number of crosses in the empty column (column 4) is:

A. 0
B. 1
C. 2
D. 3

parsed.stem:
In the 4 x 4 array shown below, each cell of the first three columns has either a cross (X) or a number, as per the given rule.

Rule: The number in a cell represents the count of crosses around its immediate neighboring cells (left, right, top, bottom, diagonals).

The array (rows top to bottom, columns left to right) is:
Row 1: 1 | 1 | 2 | ?
Row 2: 1 | X | 3 | ?
Row 3: 2 | X | 4 | ?
Row 4: X | 1 | X | ?

As per this rule, the maximum number of crosses in the empty column (column 4) is:

sourceQuestion:
The assembly shown below has three teethed circular objects (Pinions) and two teethed flat objects (Racks), which are perfectly mating with each other. Pinions can only rotate clockwise or anti-clockwise staying at its own center. Racks can translate towards the left (->) or the right (->) direction.

The assembly (from the figure): Pinion 1 meshes with Rack 1 on top and Rack 2 below. Pinion 2 meshes with Rack 2. Pinion 3 meshes with Rack 2. Rack 1 is on top, Rack 2 is in the middle between pinions.

If the object A (Rack) is translating towards the right (+) direction, the correct statement among the following is:

A. Object B translates towards the right direction.
B. Object B translates towards the left direction.
C. Object C rotates in the anti-clockwise direction.
D. Object C rotates in the clockwise direction.
sourceAnswer: B. Object B translates towards the left direction.

parsed.stem:
The assembly shown below has three teethed circular objects (Pinions) and two teethed flat objects (Racks), which are perfectly mating with each other. Pinions can only rotate clockwise or anti-clockwise staying at its own center. Racks can translate towards the left (->) or the right (->) direction.

The assembly (from the figure): Pinion 1 meshes with Rack 1 on top and Rack 2 below. Pinion 2 meshes with Rack 2. Pinion 3 meshes with Rack 2. Rack 1 is on top, Rack 2 is in the middle between pinions.

If the object A (Rack) is translating towards the right (+) direction, the correct statement among the following is:

options.text: Object B translates towards the right direction.
Object B translates towards the left direction.
Object C rotates in the anti-clockwise direction.
Object C rotates in the clockwise direction.

sourceQuestion:
A palindrome is a word that reads the same forwards and backwards. In a game of words, a player has the following two plates painted with letters:

  Plate 1: A    Plate 2: D

From the additional plates given in the options, which one of the combinations of additional plates would allow the player to construct a five-letter palindrome? The player should use all five plates exactly once. The plates can be rotated in their plane.

A. Q I Z
B. D I A
C. M I W
D. I I Z

parsed.stem:
A palindrome is a word that reads the same forwards and backwards. In a game of words, a player has the following two plates painted with letters:

  Plate 1: A    Plate 2: D

From the additional plates given in the options, which one of the combinations of additional plates would allow the player to construct a five-letter palindrome? The player should use all five plates exactly once. The plates can be rotated in their plane.

options.text: Q I Z
D I A
M I W
I I Z

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the options best describes the transformation of the 2-dimensional figure P to Q, and then to R, as shown?

(The figure shows an asymmetric arrow-like shape. P is the original. Q is obtained from P, then R is obtained from Q.)

A. Option_A: A clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a horizontal line
B. Option_B: A counter clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a horizontal line
C. Option_C: A clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a vertical line
D. Option_D: A counter clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a vertical line
sourceAnswer: A. Option_A: A clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a horizontal line

parsed.stem:
Which one of the options best describes the transformation of the 2-dimensional figure P to Q, and then to R, as shown?

(The figure shows an asymmetric arrow-like shape. P is the original. Q is obtained from P, then R is obtained from Q.)

options.text: Option_A: A clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a horizontal line
Option_B: A counter clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a horizontal line
Option_C: A clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a vertical line
Option_D: A counter clockwise rotation by 90 degrees about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the figure; A reflection along a vertical line

sourceQuestion:
Consider a circle with its centre at the origin (O), as shown. Two operations are allowed on the circle.

Operation 1: Scale independently along the x and y axes.
Operation 2: Rotation in any direction about the origin.

Which figure among the options can be achieved through a combination of these two operations on the given circle?

A. An ellipse with its centre at the origin and its major axis along a diagonal (not aligned with x or y axis)
B. An ellipse with its centre not at the origin
C. A circle with its centre not at the origin
D. An ellipse with its centre at the origin and its major axis along the x or y axis
sourceAnswer: A. An ellipse with its centre at the origin and its major axis along a diagonal (not aligned with x or y axis)

parsed.stem:
Consider a circle with its centre at the origin (O), as shown. Two operations are allowed on the circle.

Operation 1: Scale independently along the x and y axes.
Operation 2: Rotation in any direction about the origin.

Which figure among the options can be achieved through a combination of these two operations on the given circle?

options.text: An ellipse with its centre at the origin and its major axis along a diagonal (not aligned with x or y axis)
An ellipse with its centre not at the origin
A circle with its centre not at the origin
An ellipse with its centre at the origin and its major axis along the x or y axis

A. 120 degrees
B. 60 degrees
C. 90 degrees
D. 180 degrees

sourceQuestion:

[View 1: top=5, front=4, right=2] [View 2: top=6, front=5, right=4] [View 3: top=1, front=5, right=6]

The piece of paper that can be folded to make this dice is

A. [Net showing: top row 5,1,4 with 6 below 1 and 2 to the right of 4]
B. [Net showing: top row 5,4,3 with 6 below 4 and 2 to the right of 5]
C. [Net showing: top row 4,6,5 with 1 below 6 and 2 below 5]
D. [Net showing: top row 5,1,2 with 4 below 1 and 3 to the right of 5]

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following square with the four corners and the center marked as P, Q, R, S and T respectively.

[Square: P top-left, Q top-right, R bottom-right, S bottom-left, T center]

Let X, Y and Z represent the following operations:
X: rotation of the square by 180 degrees with respect to the S-Q axis.
Y: rotation of the square by 180 degrees with respect to the P-R axis.
Z: rotation of the square by 90 degrees clockwise with respect to the axis perpendicular to the square and passing through the point T.

Consider the following three distinct sequences of operation (which are applied in the left to right order):
1. XYZ
2. XY
3. ZZZZ

Which one of the following statements is correct as per the information provided above?

A. The sequence of operations (1) and (2) are equivalent
B. The sequence of operations (1) and (3) are equivalent
C. The sequence of operations (2) and (3) are equivalent
D. The sequence of operations (1), (2) and (3) are equivalent
sourceAnswer: B. The sequence of operations (1) and (3) are equivalent

parsed.stem:
Consider the following square with the four corners and the center marked as P, Q, R, S and T respectively.

[Square: P top-left, Q top-right, R bottom-right, S bottom-left, T center]

Let X, Y and Z represent the following operations:
X: rotation of the square by 180 degrees with respect to the S-Q axis.
Y: rotation of the square by 180 degrees with respect to the P-R axis.
Z: rotation of the square by 90 degrees clockwise with respect to the axis perpendicular to the square and passing through the point T.

Consider the following three distinct sequences of operation (which are applied in the left to right order):
1. XYZ
2. XY
3. ZZZZ

Which one of the following statements is correct as per the information provided above?

options.text: The sequence of operations (1) and (2) are equivalent
The sequence of operations (1) and (3) are equivalent
The sequence of operations (2) and (3) are equivalent
The sequence of operations (1), (2) and (3) are equivalent

The given figure consists of 35 unit squares arranged as shown. In addition to the three black squares, what is the minimum number of squares that must be coloured black so that both PQ and MN are lines of symmetry? (The figure is representative.)

A. 3
B. 4
C. 5
D. 6

sourceQuestion:
The word TRIANGLE is written in the first quadrant (above the X-axis) of a coordinate plane. The mirror image of the above text about the X-axis is

A. TRIANGLE written upside-down and reversed left-to-right (both horizontally and vertically flipped)
B. TRIANGLE written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertically flipped)
C. TRIANGLE written upside-down with partial reversal
D. TRIANGLE written as a horizontal mirror with letters reversed
sourceAnswer: B. TRIANGLE written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertically flipped)

parsed.stem: The word TRIANGLE is written in the first quadrant (above the X-axis) of a coordinate plane. The mirror image of the above text about the X-axis is

options.text: TRIANGLE written upside-down and reversed left-to-right (both horizontally and vertically flipped)
TRIANGLE written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertically flipped)
TRIANGLE written upside-down with partial reversal
TRIANGLE written as a horizontal mirror with letters reversed

sourceQuestion:
The word PHYLAXIS is written in the first quadrant (above the X-axis) of a coordinate plane. The mirror image of the above text about the x-axis is

A. PHYLAXIS written upside-down and reversed left-to-right (both flips)
B. PHYLAXIS written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertical flip)
C. PHYLAXIS written upside-down with only some letters reversed
D. PHYLAXIS written as horizontal mirror with letters reversed right-to-left
sourceAnswer: B. PHYLAXIS written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertical flip)

sourceQuestion:
For the picture shown above (a cluster of geometric polygon shapes to the left of a vertical dotted mirror line), which one of the following is the correct picture representing reflection with respect to the mirror shown as the dotted line?

A. The same cluster of shapes mirrored to the right side of the dotted line (left-right flipped)
B. The same cluster of shapes placed to the right but only partially flipped
C. The cluster rotated 90 degrees and placed to the right of the dotted line
D. The cluster placed below the dotted line
sourceAnswer: A. The same cluster of shapes mirrored to the right side of the dotted line (left-right flipped)

sourceQuestion:
A circular sheet of paper is folded along the lines in the directions shown. The paper, after being punched in the final folded state as shown and unfolded in the reverse order of folding, will look like _______.

A. [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre]
B. [Circle with two rectangular holes in the upper portion arranged horizontally]
C. [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre — mirror variant]
D. [Circle with one tall rectangular hole in the centre]
sourceAnswer: A. [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre]

options.text: [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre]
[Circle with two rectangular holes in the upper portion arranged horizontally]
[Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre — mirror variant]
[Circle with one tall rectangular hole in the centre]
